access.anon: Can browse all public information; further actions are not available before entering the sign-in flow.
access.user: Can subscribe to sections, manage todos, mark homework completion, export iCal, pin frequent links, save bus preferences, post comments/replies/reactions and upload attachments, and manage personal profile and OAuth connections.
access.admin: Can review comments, maintain descriptions, manage users, suspend accounts, manage OAuth clients, maintain bus data, and handle user content and platform security issues.
access.agent: Can only access restricted capabilities after OAuth authorization; does not inherit admin permissions by default; REST and MCP for the same abstract endpoint maintain consistent fields and permissions.

rules.sign-in-providers: Sign-in supports USTC, GitHub, and Google OAuth; custom username/password login is not considered due to security and maintenance cost.
rules.passkey-server-boundary: Better Auth passkey registration requires an existing fresh session. Its RP ID comes from the configured canonical origin; verification accepts only explicitly configured canonical/public origins whose host is the RP ID or its subdomain. Localhost and 127.0.0.1 are separate RPs and are not mixed. The legacy Authenticator table is not reused.
rules.passkey-user-flow: The sign-in page offers a WebAuthn passkey ceremony when supported. Signed-in users list, add, rename, and delete their own passkeys in linked-account settings through the Better Auth client plugin. Unsupported browsers, cancelled ceremonies, stale registration sessions, empty lists, and request failures have explicit user-visible states.
rules.debug-password-boundary: Email/password authentication is enabled only for local development or the explicit non-production E2E debug mode; production does not expose debug password authentication.
rules.oauth-callback-integrity: On a canonical same-origin deployment, OAuth login callbacks must complete state validation and session creation directly, without losing state due to proxy wrapping or container-internal host differences.
rules.welcome-flow-required: New users who have not set a name or username on first login must complete the welcome flow before proceeding.
rules.welcome-completion-resume: If the welcome flow was reached from an app-relative callbackUrl, successful completion returns to that page; otherwise completion returns home.
rules.auth-callback-not-intercepted: Authorization callback continuation requests carrying OAuth code/error and state must be allowed to complete the protocol redirect; the welcome page interceptor must not break the authorization result redirect.
rules.post-login-redirect: After successful login, the user should be redirected back to the originally requested app-relative page whenever possible; unsafe external callbacks fall back to the home page.
rules.oauth-login-resume: If an OAuth authorization request lands on /account/sign-in with raw authorization query parameters instead of callbackUrl, the sign-in flow should resume that exact authorization request after login rather than falling back to /.
rules.oauth-consent-loopback-continuation: The OAuth consent step must continue working for loopback public clients even when the client uses 127.0.0.1 and the app itself is being browsed via localhost; consent submission should preserve the original authorization query and complete the redirect back to the registered loopback callback.
rules.public-identity-display: Public identity defaults to displaying name or username, not internal identifiers.
rules.public-profile-canonical-route: When a profile has a username, /community/users/$identifier permanently redirects to the canonical /community/users/$identifier page. Profiles without a username remain reachable by ID without rendering the raw ID as primary metadata.
rules.account-deletion-retention: Settings account deletion runs through a transaction that anonymizes retained audit rows and issued suspension moderation records. Deleting an admin account is allowed only when at least one other admin remains.
rules.oauth-authorization-management: Settings separates linked sign-in accounts from applications authorized to call Life@USTC on the user's behalf. The canonical /account/settings/authorizations page lists safe client metadata and lets only the owning signed-in user revoke a grant.

fields: OAuth provider buttons: GitHub, Google, OIDC/USTC
Passkey sign-in button with unsupported, cancelled, and failure states
External or protocol-relative callbackUrl values are rejected
Sign-in action links include the current path and query as callbackUrl
Fallback to home page if no origin

routes.returns: { id, email, name, image, username, isAdmin, createdAt, updatedAt }
notes: Returns the authenticated user's core profile fields.
The path identifier accepts either a username or user ID and returns the same public profile hierarchy as /community/users/$identifier.

tools.name: account_profile_get
tools.returns: { id, email, username, name, image, isAdmin, createdAt, updatedAt }

tools.name: community_user_get
notes: Accepts exactly one lookup key: identifier (username or user ID).

fields: Keyboard- and touch-accessible horizontally scrollable contribution history
user passkeys with add, rename, and delete controls
Fresh-session and WebAuthn support states for passkey registration
Authorized OAuth applications: client display name, public URI, disabled state, scopes, last authorization time, and revoke action
Compact responsive section navigation with active-section description
Navigation links to sections and comment guide
Visually separated danger region and account deletion confirmation interface
